LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT BRAD ROTH'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COMPLAINT DEMONSTRATES PLAINTIFF SHANE O'ROURKE'S CLAIMS AND THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT CONCERNS THE MANAGEMENT OF REVELRY RESTAURANT GROUP, LLC AND MR. O'ROURKE'S EXCLUSION FROM PARTICIPATING IN THE MANAGEMENT OF THE LLC. (COMPLAINT PARS. 18, 29-33, 39, 49-50, 72, 74.) MR. ROTH, JOINED BY DEFENDANTS JORDAN COHEN AND REVELRY RESTAURNT GROUP, LLC HAS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW AT THIS JUNCTURE THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE PROSECUTION OF MR. O'ROURKE'S CLAIMS WILL BENEFIT THE LLC OR ITS MEMBERS. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED WITH THIS MOTION TENDS TO SHOW THAT MR. ROTH WAS THE SOLE MANAGER OF THE LLC AND THEREFORE, AT THIS STAGE, MR. O'ROURKE'S CLAIMS REGARDING HIS PARTICIPATION IN THE MANAGEMENT OF THE LLC ARE NOT LIKELY TO BENEFIT THE LLC OR ITS MEMBERS. THE COURT DETERMINES A SECURITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $25,000 IS WARRANTED WHICH MR. O'ROURKE MUST POST. A RULING ON THIS MOTION IS NOT A DETERMINATION OF ANY ISSUE IN THIS ACTION OR OF THE MERITS OF THE ACTION. (CAL. COPR. CODE SEC. 17709.02(C)(2).) ADDITIONALLY, THERE WILL BE A TEN DAY STAY COMMENCING WITH TODAY'S DATE. A BOND NEEDS TO BE POSTED BY SEPTEMBER 18, 2017 - 25 CALENDAR DAYS FROM TODAY. DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL SEPTEMBER 5TH TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHARLENE P. KIESSELBACH, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216, GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. =(302/CPK)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT JORDAN COHEN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JORDAN COHEN'S DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH, SIXTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF SHANE O'ROURKE'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. AN ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP CREATES A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP. MR. O'ROURKE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT HE HAD AN ATTORNEY CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH MR. COHEN AND THOSE ALLEGATIONS, WHICH MUST BE ACCEPTED AS TRUE ON THIS DEMURRER, SUPPORT THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. MR. COHEN'S ARGUMENTS THAT HE MERELY ACTED FOR THE LLC AND THAT HE WAS ONLY A SCRIVENER ARE FACTUAL ISSUES THAT ARE NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON A DEMURRER. THE SIXTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACT AND PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THERE IS A DISPUTED FACTUAL ISSUE REGARDING WHETHER MR. COHEN WAS ACTING AS AN AGENT OR IN SOME OTHER CAPACITY AND THIS ISSUE ALSO CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY DEMURRER. (ASAHI KASEI PHARMA CORP. V. ACTELION LTD. (2013) 222 CAL.APP.4TH 945, 963-964.) BASED ON THE EMAIL OF MARCH 2, 2018 FROM MR. BLUTE, THE COURT CONFIRMS THE FOREGOING AS THE ORDER ON THIS DEMURRER. COUNSEL FOR MR. O'ROURKE IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE FOREGOING LANGUAGE EXCEPT FOR THE IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING SENTENCE AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Receiver
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Continued To Apr-30-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. of Dimitriou & Associates, PC, T:415.434.1144, for plaintiff/moving party;
Andrew Kingsdale, Esq. of the Law Offices of Andrew Kingsdale, T:415.548.1950, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

PLAINTIFF SHANE O'ROURKE'S Motion For Appointment Of A Receiver is DENIED and the hearing is continued to April 30, 2018 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer regarding the terms of a proposed injunction and/or attachment protecting the rights of Mr. O'Rourke with regard to Revelry Restaurant L.L.C. Mr. O'Rourke is precluded from obtaining a receiver for Revelry on grounds that the corporation is in imminent danger of insolvency because Mr. O'Rourke is no longer suing derivatively. CCP 564(b)(6) does not authorize the appointment of a receiver for such grounds when the lawsuit is by a private person. Mr. O'Rourke has also failed to present sufficient evidence to show that Mr. Roth has been mismanaging and/or looting Revelry while failing to loan repayments to Mr. O'Rourke. The complaint and declarations in support of Mr. O'Rourke's motion are devoid of factual evidence to support the conclusory allegations. (Mason v. San-Val Oil & Water Co. (1934) 1 Cal.2d 670, 673.) The declaration of Mr. Medaglia, presents detailed evidence that Mr. Roth has not been mismanaging or looting the LLC and that there is no actual or threatened cessation of business operations to warrant receivership. The appointment of a receiver is also inappropriate because there appear to be less severe remedies that can adequately protect Mr. O'Rourke's rights as a shareholder and creditor, such as an injunction or attachment. (Golden State Glass Corp. v. Superior Court of Los Angeles Cty. (1939) 13 Cal.2d 384, 393.) The parties are required to meet and confer no later than April 20, 2018 in a good faith effort to reach agreement on the terms of a proposed injunction and/or attachment. If they are unable to reach agreement, no later than April 24, 2018 each side may submit to the court his proposed order(s) accompanied by a memorandum or no more than five pages explaining why the court should sign the proposed order(s).

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JORDAN COHEN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Jordan Cohen's demurrer to the fifth, sixth and eighth causes of action in plaintiff Shane O'Rourke's first amended complaint is overruled as to all three causes of action. An attorney-client relationship creates a fiduciary relationship. Mr. O'Rourke sufficiently alleges that he had an attorney client relationship with Mr. Cohen and those allegations, which must be accepted as true on this demurrer, support the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Mr. Cohen's arguments that he merely acted for the LLC and that he was only a scrivener are factual issues that are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The sixth and eighth causes of action for interference with contract and prospective economic advantage are adequately alleged. There is a disputed factual issue regarding whether Mr. Cohen was acting as an agent or in some other capacity and this issue also cannot be resolved by demurrer. (Asahi Kasei Pharma Corp. v. Actelion Ltd. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945, 963-964.) Based on the email of March 2, 2018 from Mr. Blute, the court confirms the foregoing as the order on this demurrer. Counsel for Mr. O'Rourke is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the foregoing language except for the immediately preceding sentence and submit it to the court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. 415-434-1144 of Dimitriou & Associates for Plaintiff Shane O'Rourke.
Jonathan Blute, Esq. 415-788-1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feenye for Defendant JORDAN REIS COHEN.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRAD ROTH's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF SHANE O'ROURKE'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Brad Roth's demurrer to the first, second, fourth and sixth through tenth causes of action in plaintiff Shane O'Rourke's first amended complaint is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to sixth, eighth, ninth and tenth causes for interference with contract and prospective economic advantage, declaratory relief and conversion and b) overruled as to all other causes of action. Mr. O'Rourke sufficiently alleges consideration and the terms of the contract for his first cause of action for breach of Membership Sales Agreement. The reduction of the number of LLC members benefitted Mr. Roth. In addition, Mr. O'Rourke as promisee was prejudiced by paying the $15,000. Each is sufficient consideration. (Steiner v. Thexton (2010) 48 Cal.4th 411, 420-421.) The allegations that Mr. Roth cut off access to LLC information adequately supports the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Liberally construed, the fourth cause of action for breach of implied covenant is based on both the operating agreement and Membership Sales Agreement. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. O'Rourke has leave to amend his sixth cause of action for interference with contract that Mr. Roth intended to interfere with the loan agreement. The disputed factual issue whether Mr. Roth was acting for the LLC or for himself is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. (Asahi Kasei Pharma Corp. v. Actelion Ltd. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 945, 963-964.) Mr. O'Rourke sufficiently alleges the terms of the contract according to their legal intendment and effect to overcome the demurrer to the seventh cause of action for breach of the operating agreement. Mr. O'Rourke has leave to allege an economic relationship between himself and a third party to support his eighth cause of action for interference with prospective economic advantage. Mr. O'Rourke has leave to clearly allege the respective ownership interests on his ninth cause of action for declaratory relief. Mr. O'Rourke is given leave to amend to clarify what sums were allegedly taken from him to support his tenth cause of action for conversion. If Mr. O'Rourke is complaining about sums taken from the LLC, Mr. O'Rourke must allege a derivative claim.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In Defendant Brad Roths Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar Aug-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Dimitrou, Esq. (415-434-1144) of Dimitriou & Associates for Plaintiff/Opposing party Shane O'Rourke.
Andrew Kingsdale, Esq. (415-548-1950) for Defendant/moving party Brad Roth.
Jonathan Blute, Esq. and Whitney Carlton (415-788-1900) of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant Jordan Cohen
Nancy Chung, Esq. (415-399-9301) law offices of David Schwartz for Defendant Revelry Restaurant Group, LLC

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with the following modification as to the timing and stay as follows:

DEFENDANT JORDAN COHEN's Joinder In Defendant Brad Roths Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security

Defendant Brad Roth's motion for order requiring plaintiff to furnish security is granted. The complaint demonstrates plaintiff Shane O'Rourke's claims and the gravamen of the complaint concerns the management of Revelry Restaurant Group, LLC and Mr. O'Rourke's exclusion from participating in the management of the LLC. (Complaint Pars. 18, 29-33, 39, 49-50, 72, 74.) Mr. Roth, joined by defendants Jordan Cohen and Revelry Restaurnt Group, LLC has provided sufficient evidence to show at this juncture there is no reasonable possibility that the prosecution of Mr. O'Rourke's claims will benefit the LLC or its members. The evidence presented with this motion tends to show that Mr. Roth was the sole manager of the LLC and therefore, at this stage, Mr. O'Rourke's claims regarding his participation in the management of the LLC are not likely to benefit the LLC or its members. The court determines a security in the amount of $25,000 is warranted which Mr. O'Rourke must post. A ruling on this motion is not a determination of any issue in this action or of the merits of the action. (Cal. Copr. Code Sec. 17709.02(c)(2).)

Additionally, there will be a ten day stay commencing with today's date. A bond needs to be posted by September 18, 2017 - 25 calendar days from today. Defendant has until September 5th to respond to the Complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
